# Successor evaluation predeclaration checkpoint

This directory holds the frozen evaluation inputs for the mvdan.cc/sh/v3 successor helper
evaluation, as defined by section 8 of
[the mvdan helper evaluation design spec](../../../docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-21-mvdan-helper-evaluation-design.md).
It is the branch's first post-design, pre-implementation reviewed commit; the design
document precedes it, and no helper, wheel, or gate code exists yet. Every file here is an
input the eventual implementation is measured against, not a result of running it.

## Immutability contract (S8)

`MANIFEST.sha256` covers inputs only and is never repinned. A post-freeze change creates a
new checkpoint revision and invalidates the entire evaluation: gate evidence, being keyed to
the checkpoint digest, is discarded and the evaluation restarts from the new checkpoint
commit. Gate results therefore live outside this directory and are never manifested here.
`MANIFEST.sha256` is generated by `scripts/successor_checkpoint_manifest.py` and verified by
`tests/test_successor_checkpoint.py::test_manifest_matches_checkpoint_inputs`, which shares
its walk and line format with the generator so the two can never disagree. The sibling
`test_frozen_d3_checkpoint_untouched` proves this checkpoint never mutates the frozen D3
`github_ci_checkpoint/`.

## Artifact groups

### `pins/`

External identity pins for the evaluation: the Go toolchain (exact version and a per-builder
archive sha256 for all five builders), the `mvdan.cc/sh/v3` v3.13.1 parser with module and
go.mod hashes, the bash 5.2.21 and shfmt 3.13.1 differential oracles with container digest,
binary sha256, and exact command lines, the CI action revisions pinned to full commit SHAs,
and the platform matrix. `ci_actions_tags.json` records the human-readable tag each action
SHA resolved from, and `platform_matrix.json` freezes the five target triples, wheel tags,
and runner labels.

### `tables/`

The certifier's frozen decision tables: the certified-construct table walked over the parser
AST, the reason-code-to-scope table with stable reasons and D4 mappings, the dispatcher
grammar, the policy precedence chain, and the pre-policy command matrix. These are the
successor grammar's ground truth; the certifier certifies or refuses exactly what these
tables say and nothing more.

### `protocol/`

The helper wire protocol: the strict draft-2020-12 JSON Schema (protocol_version 1, all
objects closed), the canonical encoder rules, the digest-input manifest that defines the
helper-identity hash, positive conformance fixtures, and raw negative fixtures enumerating
every rejection the schema must catch. These freeze the byte-exact contract between the
Python engine and the Go helper before either is written. The conformance fixtures'
`helper_version` is a synthetic 64-hex placeholder and their `work_units` values are wire-shape
data for byte-exact marshal tests, not values derived from the `limits.json` work-unit
definition.

### `corpus/`

The re-derived acceptance corpus: `acceptance_labels.json` labels every one of the 87
acceptance rows `must-certify`, `intentional-exit-2`, or `outside-direct-marker-contract`
with a per-row derivation, and `new_fixtures.json` adds the seven successor fixture families
(dispatcher, look-alike, heredoc guard, malformed tail, offset oracle, StmtsSeq, encoder
composition). `relabel_report.md` is the human-readable re-derivation produced by
`scripts/derive_successor_labels.py`, byte-identical on identical inputs.

### `tiers/`

Predeclared gate expectations: Tier 1 pins the exact managed-workflow findings, Tier 2 pins
the checked-in PR-workflow pruning outcome, Tier 3A re-derives every documented D3
conformance case, and Tier 3B predeclares the 20 envelope-fixture statuses. Predeclaring
these before the helper exists is what makes the eventual gate run a falsifiable test rather
than a fit to observed behavior.

### `limits.json`, `budgets.json`, `tripwires.json`

`limits.json` freezes every cap and formula from sections 3.5 and 4.4 (source, request, and
output caps, the deadline formula, the visitor and statement caps, work-unit definitions, and
the section 9 performance and RSS ceilings). `budgets.json` records the eight gate predicates
and the Tier 3B envelope budget; `tripwires.json` records the owned-surface, net-reduction,
binary, wheel, execution-count, and retention limits that decide whether the successor is
adopted.

### `legacy_normalization.json`

The legacy-reason normalization artifact (S6.4), pinned at deletion baseline `be4b7b1`. It
maps all 580 replay-inventory entries to normalized successor tuples so the future runtime
helper's replay output can be compared against a frozen expectation with zero inference at
compare time.

## Build-container rationale (Task 2)

`platform_matrix.json` records `build_container: null` for all five targets. This is
deliberate, not a placeholder: every target builds on a native GitHub-hosted runner image
(`ubuntu-24.04`, `ubuntu-24.04-arm`, `macos-15-intel`, `macos-15`, `windows-2025`, all
cross-checked against the current GitHub-hosted runner list on 2026-07-21), so the pinned
`runner_label` is the build environment's identity and no manylinux or other build-container
digest applies. If a future revision moves any target into a container, that target's
`build_container` gains a `sha256:` digest and, per S8, the change is a new checkpoint
revision.

## Ratification list

The checkpoint carries the judgment calls the owner ratifies at checkpoint review. Two were
ratified mid-flight by Rick on 2026-07-21 and are recorded here as decided, for the record;
the remainder are presented for ratification.

### Ratified by the owner on 2026-07-21

- **Assignment-value traversal.** After the Tier 3B predeclaration adjudication, Rick
  ratified traversing the value of an `Assign` node (the `(Assign, value)` role). This flips
  acceptance corpus row 10 (assignment-only substitution) from a refusal to must-certify and
  is folded into `scripts/derive_successor_labels.py` and the frozen construct table. It
  resolves the former uncertain-container row 10.
- **Literal-assignment-prefix certification with dynamic refusal.** Rick ratified certifying
  a command whose only assignment prefixes carry statically known literal values, while a
  command carrying a dynamic assignment prefix still refuses with `assignment-prefix` and
  retains its argv. This amended S5.2 in the spec (amendment commit on this branch) and was
  evaluated against every acceptance row: it flips zero rows, because no acceptance row
  carries a literal-assignment-prefix-plus-argv marker command. The pre-policy matrix row
  `assignments-plus-argv-dynamic` records the retained-argv refusal.

### Presented for ratification at checkpoint review

- **Tripwire numbers (`tripwires.json`, `"ratified": false`).** Owned production surface at
  most 2,200 lines; net production reduction at least 1,400 lines against the 3,704-line
  deletion baseline (`shell_scanner.py` 3,031 plus `direct_marker_scanner.py` 673); helper
  binary at most 12 MiB; platform wheel at most 16 MiB; at most 5 CI native-target
  executions; 7-day artifact retention. Ratifying the checkpoint flips `"ratified"` to true,
  which per S8 is a new checkpoint revision: `MANIFEST.sha256` is regenerated in that same
  amendment commit and the revision is noted here.
- **Tier 3B zero-headroom position.** The Tier 3B envelope budget is at most 2 of 20
  indeterminate with false-positive and false-safe exactly zero. The predeclared expectations
  already sit at 2 of 20 indeterminate (fixtures 05 and 14), so there is zero headroom; any
  additional indeterminate fixture fails the gate.
- **Fixture-14 provisional category.** Tier 3B fixture 14's reason category is marked
  provisional in the frozen artifact: its indeterminate status is firm and counts against the
  budget, but the specific reason-category label is pending confirmation against the runtime
  helper. Fixture 05 (splitting-unsafe-word) is firm.
- **Acceptance-corpus adjudication rows (Task 6, cap 12, zero headroom).** The acceptance
  corpus carries exactly 12 `owner_adjudicate` rows (the cap): rows 1, 32, 37, 38, 49, 50,
  51, 60, 61, 62, 63, 71. The cap has zero headroom; a thirteenth judgment call fails the
  corpus validator.
- **Legacy normalization adjudications (Task 9, 4 group decisions, 113 entries).** The 580
  legacy entries carry 113 `owner_adjudicate` entries, all category-label-only, resolved as 4
  group decisions over the tied legacy strings: env-split-string (62 entries, pinned to
  `unsupported-construct`), command-position expansion (42 entries, pinned to
  `unquoted-expansion-in-command-word`), extglob expansion (5 entries, pinned to
  `unsupported-construct`), and dynamic-relative doc-lattice executable (4 entries, pinned to
  `policy-unresolvable`). Per-entry provenance lives in the `_ADJUDICATED` frozenset of
  `scripts/normalize_legacy_reasons.py`, not in the artifact JSON.
- **Uncertain-container rows (Task 6).** Rows 24, 25, 27, 34, 35, 36 refuse reading their
  container today; they flip to must-certify if the ratified traversal convention is later
  found to traverse those containers. (Row 10 was in this set and is now resolved by the
  ratified assignment-value traversal above.)
- **Here-string mapping (Task 6).** Rows 62, 63, 71 map the here-string
  `Redirect`/target-word-expansion behavior; the owner confirms this mapping.
- **Process-substitution refusal (Task 6).** Rows 64, 65, 66 record an explicit nod that
  `ProcSubst` refuses.
- **Lexical-category fold (Task 3).** The parser's lexical error categories are folded into
  `syntax-error` and `parser-divergence-guard` rather than carrying their own reason codes;
  the owner ratifies this interpretation. Task 3 also flagged 7 uncertain fail-closed rows
  where the certifier's gates surface a refusal; those are ratified with the fold.
- **bash `version_full` minor and `_tags` sibling file (Task 2).** `bash_pin.json` normalizes
  the version to `"5.2.21"` (dropping D3's `(1)-release` suffix) to satisfy the exact-match
  validator; the raw D3 string is not otherwise retained, and a `version_full` key is offered
  as an optional addition if later oracle tooling needs it. CI action tag provenance lives in
  the sibling `pins/ci_actions_tags.json` rather than a `_tags` key inside `ci_actions.json`,
  because the verbatim validator loops over every value in `ci_actions.json` as a 40-hex SHA.
- **S3.1 plan-deviation note (Task 7).** The plan's Task 7 expected the
  linear-then-unterminated-subshell fixture to retain `[["linear", false]]`. The spec (S3.1)
  governs: an incomplete top-level `BinaryCmd` yields nothing, so the frozen expectation is
  `[]`, verified against the pinned parser source. The spec was followed over the plan.

## Revisions

- **2026-07-21 (pre-review): multibyte-assignment-prefix alignment.** The `offset_oracle`
  family's `multibyte-assignment-prefix` fixture (`corpus/new_fixtures.json`) was rewritten
  before the owner checkpoint review to align with the ratified literal-assignment-prefix-
  with-dynamic-refusal amendment (amendment B, see Ratification list above): the assignment
  value changed from the all-literal `VAR="naïve"`, which now certifies under that amendment,
  to the dynamic `VAR="naïve $X"`, so the fixture continues to exercise an assignment-prefix
  refusal. `expected_refusal_raw_index` (13) is unchanged since the prefix up to `VAR` is
  unchanged. `MANIFEST.sha256` was regenerated in the same revision.

## Handoff

Do not push, open a PR, or start implementation from this checkpoint. Implementation planning
begins only after the owner approves the checkpoint and ratifies the tripwires (S10), recorded
by flipping `tripwires.json` `"ratified"` to true in a review-approved amendment commit, which
per S8 is a new checkpoint revision that regenerates `MANIFEST.sha256` in the same commit.
